meta.name: Aadhav S. Bharadwaj
meta.title: Aadhav Bharadwaj — LLM Systems & Full-Stack
meta.description: CS & Math @ CWRU. Production LLM infrastructure, hybrid RAG, eval pipelines, and shipped full-stack systems.
meta.thesis: LLM systems you can trace and ship

experience.label: Experience
experience.note: One yellow — internships and jobs on the sky

project.label: Project
project.note: One purple — repos and shipped systems (all project tiers share this hue)

rules: Size = tier (featured big, gallery medium, lab small)
Color = starKind (yellow experience OR purple project) — not constellation hue
A featured internship is ONE star: big + yellow (not a yellow branch off a blue project node)
Gallery and lab stars use the same purple as featured projects, smaller radius only

skills.label: Python
skills.writeup: Primary language across LLM agents, eval pipelines, research notebooks, and teaching tooling at CWRU. Powers Blackbox-QA's retrieval stack, Abeyon's parallel NDJSON batch jobs, and Python autograding utilities used in weekly labs for 100+ students.

skills.label: TypeScript
skills.writeup: Typed front-end and API contracts for production web apps — kiosk UI, debate platform, and multi-site inventory tooling. Keeps large Next.js codebases maintainable with strict schemas at component and route boundaries.

skills.label: JavaScript
skills.writeup: Runtime for real-time signaling, MERN services, and browser WebRTC clients across Arguably and internship platforms. Used wherever fast iteration on event-driven UI and Node backends mattered more than static typing.

skills.label: Java
skills.writeup: Enterprise service layer work during the Abeyon internship — retrieval fusion utilities, Elasticsearch integration, and batch generation controllers in existing Java codebases. Bridges Python eval tooling with production JVM services.

skills.label: SQL
skills.writeup: Read-only SQL tool paths in Blackbox-QA and schema design for pgvector-backed retrieval stores. Also used for labeled eval sets, gold-set fixtures, and structured logging queries behind Langfuse dashboards.

skills.label: R
skills.writeup: Statistical analysis and coursework visualization during the CS & Math curriculum at CWRU. Supports data-heavy assignments rather than production portfolio systems.

skills.label: RAG
skills.writeup: Retrieval-augmented generation from internship gateways to personal eval harnesses — hybrid BM25 + dense fusion, Pinecone on the kiosk, and MERN vector search at Eaton. The through-line of the portfolio: measure recall, trace failures, ship fixes.

skills.label: Embeddings
skills.writeup: Dense retrieval arms in hybrid pipelines — embedding models behind pgvector indexes and Eaton's trainer search. Paired with BM25 and reciprocal rank fusion so sparse and semantic signals complement each other on labeled gold sets.

skills.label: LLM-as-Judge Evals
skills.writeup: Frozen gold sets scored by automated judges for citation match, answer quality, and stage-level RAG debugging. Ran 100-case WI benchmarks at Abeyon and CI-gated judge metrics on every Blackbox-QA pull request.

skills.label: Hybrid Retrieval (BM25 + RRF)
skills.writeup: BM25 plus dense retrieval fused with reciprocal rank fusion — the pattern that lifted Recall@40 to 0.887 and Recall@5 from 0.04 (BM25-only) to 0.88 on labeled sets. Standard approach across Abeyon production apps and the Blackbox-QA reference implementation.

skills.label: pgvector
skills.writeup: Postgres vector extension backing deterministic retrieval evals and production-adjacent indexes. Blackbox-QA runs pgvector-backed Recall@5 regression gates on every PR so embedding drift is caught before merge.

skills.label: PyTorch
skills.writeup: Training and experimentation stack for transformer inference research at the Datta Research Lab — chunked decomposition, KV-cache analysis, and teacher–student distillation runs. Implements ResNet-18 and ViT-Tiny compression studies in the knowledge-distillation lab repos.

skills.label: Knowledge Distillation
skills.writeup: Teacher–student compression experiments comparing ResNet-18 and ViT-Tiny under distillation loss schedules at CWRU. Sits alongside pruning and chunked-decomposition work as part of the Lab constellation on the portfolio sky.

skills.label: Computer Vision
skills.writeup: HackCWRU ExpressInk pipeline — vision models on children's drawings to support emotional-state screening. Shipped as a gallery-tier project; backend split across ExpressInk and ExpressInkBackend repos.

skills.label: Langfuse
skills.writeup: Self-hosted Langfuse v3 for company-wide cost, token, and latency attribution across nine priced models. Instrumented production FastAPI gateways and Blackbox-QA spans so every retrieval and generation step is traceable in one UI.

skills.label: vLLM
skills.writeup: On-prem inference behind the Abeyon LLM gateway before and during AWS Bedrock migration. Routed alongside Bedrock with OpenAI-compatible APIs and custom regex pricing in Langfuse.

skills.label: AWS Bedrock
skills.writeup: Managed inference target after migrating routing across three Abeyon applications from self-hosted vLLM. Part of the latency and cost optimization story alongside parallel NDJSON batch processing.

skills.label: FastAPI
skills.writeup: Production LLM gateway and agent service layer — OpenAI-compatible routes, structured logging, and Langfuse middleware. Blackbox-QA and Abeyon both treat FastAPI as the thin orchestration shell over retrieval and tool calls.

skills.label: OpenAI-Compatible Gateways
skills.writeup: Drop-in `/v1/chat/completions` surfaces so existing clients swap backends without rewrites. Abeyon's gateway abstracts vLLM and Bedrock; Blackbox-QA exposes the same pattern for local agent development.

skills.label: Next.js
skills.writeup: App-router front ends for the CWRU AI avatar kiosk, Arguably debate UI, Inventory360, and Palate. Chosen for SSR, API routes, and fast iteration on dual-screen kiosk layouts serving 100+ daily users.

skills.label: React
skills.writeup: Component model for real-time debate views, HackCWRU ExpressInk UI, and inventory dashboards. Paired with custom WebRTC media components and form-heavy admin flows.

skills.label: Node.js
skills.writeup: Signaling servers, MERN APIs, and Socket.IO backends for Arguably, Palate, and Eaton trainer workflows. Handles authoritative turn sync and trainer session analytics over WebSockets.

skills.label: Express
skills.writeup: HTTP layer for MERN stacks at Eaton and Palate — REST routes, JWT middleware, and embedding search endpoints behind the GenAI trainer chatbots.

skills.label: MongoDB
skills.writeup: Document store for Wildlife SOS Inventory360 (1,000+ users, 12 sites) and Palate's recipe corpus. Schema validated at the API boundary with Zod on the Inventory360 Next.js app.

skills.label: PostgreSQL
skills.writeup: Relational backbone for pgvector indexes, gold-set tables, and read-only SQL tool paths in retrieval agents. Preferred when eval reproducibility and ACID matter more than document flexibility.

skills.label: Prisma
skills.writeup: ORM and migration layer for Arguably's debate persistence — rooms, users, and match state behind the Next.js app. Keeps schema changes aligned with the mediasoup signaling stack.

skills.label: MERN
skills.writeup: Full-stack pattern for Eaton's GenAI trainer platform and Palate's recipe generator — MongoDB, Express, React, Node in containerized deploy with CI/CD. Eaton MERN stack improved onboarding efficiency by approximately 40%.

skills.label: Zod
skills.writeup: Runtime validation for Inventory360 API payloads and form inputs across 12 distributed conservation sites. Catches bad asset records before they hit MongoDB.

skills.label: Socket.IO
skills.writeup: Authoritative signaling and turn synchronization for Arguably's live debate engine alongside the mediasoup SFU. ~2.5k LOC of SFU + signaling infrastructure with CI and HTTPS/TURN hardening.

skills.label: WebRTC
skills.writeup: Browser transport for Arguably's peer media paths through a standalone mediasoup SFU. Custom media components integrate capture, publish, and subscribe flows with production reliability testing.

skills.label: mediasoup
skills.writeup: Selective forwarding unit at the core of Arguably — handles RTP routing, bandwidth adaptation, and multi-party debate rooms. Built as a standalone service with unit tests and TURN-backed deployment.

skills.label: Docker
skills.writeup: Containerized Blackbox-QA stacks and Eaton's MERN trainer platform for reproducible deploys. Compose-based local dev for pgvector + API services; CI/CD pipelines push images to production.

skills.label: CI/CD
skills.writeup: GitHub Actions eval gates on Blackbox-QA (Recall@5 regression) and Eaton's containerized release pipeline. Automated judge scoring and retrieval fixtures block merges when metrics slip.

skills.label: AWS Lambda
skills.writeup: Serverless orchestration behind the CWRU AI avatar kiosk — RAG lookup, HeyGen session management, and SSO-aware routing. Part of the architecture that holds end-to-end latency under 1.5 seconds.

skills.label: AWS S3
skills.writeup: Object storage for kiosk media assets and session artifacts in the WSOM deployment. Used alongside Lambda for static offload so the Next.js front end stays responsive on dual screens.

skills.label: Linux
skills.writeup: Daily driver for WSL development, Docker hosts, and tmux-based eval runs during the Abeyon internship. Shell scripting for indexer pipelines, gateway tests, and long-running benchmark sessions.

skills.label: WebSocket
skills.writeup: Live KPI dashboards for Eaton trainer sessions — real-time analytics over modular training workflows. Pushes session metrics to MERN admin views without polling.

skills.label: Pinecone
skills.writeup: Managed vector index for the CWRU AI avatar kiosk RAG layer — fast semantic lookup over university knowledge bases. Bridges The Presence constellation to The Instrument's hybrid-retrieval story.

skills.label: HeyGen
skills.writeup: Streaming avatar rendering on the dual-screen kiosk — lip-synced responses driven by RAG output. Integrated with Lambda orchestration so avatar latency stays within the sub-1.5s budget.

skills.label: GPT-4
skills.writeup: Recipe generation backbone for Palate — text and food-image prompts to structured recipes. Served 500+ generated recipes at 98% uptime on the MERN stack.

skills.label: Unity
skills.writeup: 2D platformer engine for Brick'd Up (ECSE290 team project) — inventive level mechanics shipped to Itch.io. Gallery-tier breadth project on The Shelf constellation.

skills.label: JWT / RBAC
skills.writeup: Role-based access control on Eaton's containerized trainer platform — JWT auth gates trainer vs admin routes. Supports multi-tenant training workflows with live session analytics.

projects.title: Blackbox-QA
projects.hook: Framework-free tool-calling agent over hybrid retrieval with Langfuse tracing and CI regression gates.
projects.writeup: Built a framework-free tool-calling agent over hybrid retrieval (BM25, dense embeddings, reciprocal rank fusion) and read-only SQL — achieving Recall@5 and MRR of 0.88 versus 0.04 for BM25-only retrieval on labeled gold sets. Implemented Langfuse tracing with automated LLM-as-judge scoring; reached citation match of 0.84 and answer quality of 0.856 on frozen eval fixtures. Added Recall@5 CI regression gates and deterministic pgvector-backed retrieval evaluations on every pull request so embedding or ranker changes cannot silently regress production quality.

projects.title: Abeyon
projects.subtitle: Software Engineer Intern — AI/LLM · Washington, DC · May 2026 – Present
projects.hook: Production LLM internship — hybrid RAG eval wins, 2.49× batch latency, Bedrock migration, and self-hosted Langfuse across nine models.
projects.writeup: Software Engineer Intern at Abeyon (May 2026 – Present): shipped measurable LLM infrastructure in production, not a side project with a company logo pasted on. Implemented BM25 + dense hybrid retrieval with reciprocal rank fusion, improving Recall@40 from 0.875 to 0.887 and lifting retrieval-arm MRR to 0.610 on labeled evaluation sets. Cut work-item generation latency from 123.6s to 49.7s (2.49×) via parallel NDJSON batch processing and migrated inference routing to AWS Bedrock across three applications. Deployed self-hosted Langfuse v3 with custom pricing and tracing on the FastAPI gateway (vLLM + Bedrock) so cost, token, and latency attribution work company-wide — observability is one slice of the story, not the headline.

projects.title: Arguably
projects.hook: Rules-driven debate engine with mediasoup SFU, Socket.IO turn sync, and live transcription pipeline.
projects.writeup: Implemented a standalone mediasoup SFU and Socket.IO signaling stack (~2.5k LOC) and integrated browser WebRTC functionality through custom media components for a rules-driven live debate engine. Added SFU unit testing, CI automation, HTTPS/CORS/TURN infrastructure, and production reliability improvements so multi-party rooms stay synchronized under authoritative server turn logic. Real-time infrastructure story for The Arena constellation — the counterweight to traceable batch LLM systems in The Instrument.

projects.title: CWRU AI Avatar Kiosk
projects.subtitle: Software Engineer Intern · WSOM, CWRU · Jun 2025 – Present
projects.hook: Dual-screen AI avatar kiosk with Next.js, AWS Lambda, HeyGen, and Pinecone RAG — sub-1.5s latency for 100+ daily users through CWRU SSO.
projects.writeup: Built a dual-screen AI avatar kiosk using Next.js, AWS Lambda, HeyGen, and Pinecone-backed RAG as Software Engineer Intern at WSOM, Case Western Reserve University (Jun 2025 – Present). Achieved sub-1.5s end-to-end latency while serving 100+ daily users through CWRU SSO on physical kiosk hardware. Bridges shipped product UX (The Presence) with the same RAG patterns measured in Blackbox-QA and Abeyon — proof that retrieval quality work shows up in user-facing latency budgets, not just offline benchmarks.

projects.title: Eaton GenAI Platform
projects.subtitle: AI Software Engineer Intern · Remote · Sep 2025 – Jan 2026
projects.hook: MERN RAG chatbots for customer-interaction training with containerized deploy, RBAC, and live KPI dashboards.
projects.writeup: Architected GenAI RAG chatbots for customer-interaction training using a MERN backend with vector retrieval and embedding search as AI Software Engineer Intern at Eaton Corporation (Sep 2025 – Jan 2026), improving onboarding efficiency by approximately 40%. Deployed a containerized MERN platform with CI/CD, JWT/RBAC, and live WebSocket KPI dashboards for production trainer session analytics across modular training workflows. Industry-scale GenAI in The Platform constellation — same RAG vocabulary as The Instrument, applied to trainer enablement rather than developer tooling.

projects.title: Wildlife SOS
projects.subtitle: Software Developer · Inventory360 platform · Jun 2025 – Jul 2025
projects.hook: Secure inventory management (Inventory360) for 12 distributed sites and 1,000+ users with real-time asset tracking.

projects.title: Palate
projects.hook: MERN recipe platform with GPT-4 generation from text or food images.

projects.title: ExpressInk
projects.hook: HackCWRU project using computer vision on children's drawings to support emotional-state screening.

projects.title: Brick'd Up
projects.hook: 2D Unity platformer built as an ECSE290 team project — inventive level mechanics, published on Itch.io.

projects.title: ML Research Labs
projects.hook: Chunked decomposition and distillation/pruning experiments — transformer inference research at the Datta Research Lab, CWRU.

projects.title: Knowledge Distillation Lab
projects.hook: Knowledge distillation with ResNet-18 and ViT-Tiny — teacher-student compression experiments at the Datta Research Lab, CWRU.

highlights: Deployed Langfuse v3 and instrumented production FastAPI LLM gateway with cost, token, and latency attribution across 9 models.
Built LLM-as-judge eval pipelines; improved Recall@40 via BM25 + dense hybrid retrieval with RRF.
Cut work-item generation latency 2.49× via parallel NDJSON batch processing; migrated inference to AWS Bedrock.
Architected GenAI RAG chatbots for customer-interaction training (~40% onboarding lift).
Deployed containerized MERN platform with CI/CD, JWT/RBAC, and live WebSocket KPI dashboards.
Built dual-screen AI avatar kiosk with Next.js, AWS Lambda, HeyGen, and Pinecone RAG.
Achieved sub-1.5s latency serving 100+ daily users through CWRU SSO.

experience.hook: Undergraduate research on transformer inference efficiency — distillation, pruning, KV-cache, and chunked decomposition.
highlights: Transformer inference-efficiency research — chunked decomposition, KV-cache analysis, and SVD experiments (chunkedDecomp, KV-Cache-Analysis repos).
Knowledge distillation and pruning studies with ResNet-18 and ViT-Tiny teacher–student compression (knowledge-distillation-lab, pruning-lab).
Lead weekly CS labs for 100+ students and hold 5+ office hours weekly.
Developed Python autograding utilities supporting course operations.
Built internal asset-tracking platform for 1,000+ users across 12 conservation sites.
